title: amaryllis belladonna subsp. monantha
intro: Amaryllis belladonna subsp. monantha, also known as the naked lady or belladonna lily, is a subspecies of the amaryllis belladonna plant, which is a type of flowering bulbous perennial native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of alkaloids such as scopolamine and hyoscyamine. Consuming the plant can have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or in infusions, although it should be handled with caution due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
